meaning: v.包围,环绕,包含或包括某事物
examples: They correspond roughly to small municipal, medium-sized metropolitan and major metropolitan airports respectively, and encompass an increasingly rigorous set of regulations.
The hand tool additions encompass everything from tubing cutters to nut drivers.
They built a moat to encompass the castle.
It seemed far too small to encompass so many different lives, rising and falling fortunes, history and myths.
Your enterprise-class platform must seamlessly encompass all major aspects of your business to be truly successful.
